# New OpenClaw Skills Update is INSANE!

AI Training 👉   
AI-Powered SEO System 👉 

Most people install a new AI tool, click around for a few minutes, get confused, and never use it again. Meanwhile, the people who actually understand how these systems work are building automated workflows that run tasks for them all day long. One of the biggest reasons for that difference is understanding how AI agents gain capabilities, and that’s exactly what the latest Open Claw skills update is about. [...] Installing skills has also become easier. Instead of complicated setup steps, you can install many skills with a single command. Once installed, the agent can immediately use the new capability during conversations without needing to restart the system.

Many users start with a small core set of useful skills. These typically include tools for web search, document summarization, development integrations, and connections to services like Google Workspace. Once the basic stack is installed, additional skills can be added depending on the tasks you want the agent to automate. [...] Open Claw itself is an open-source AI agent that runs locally on your computer. Instead of relying entirely on cloud platforms, it lets you operate your own AI assistant directly on your machine. It has grown extremely quickly and now has hundreds of thousands of users experimenting with local AI agents.

However, out of the box Open Claw is basically a blank system. It can chat with you, but it doesn’t have many real capabilities yet. The real power comes from something called skills. Skills are small packages that teach the agent how to perform specific tasks such as searching the web, connecting to APIs, summarizing content, or interacting with other services.

results.title: OpenClaw Application Version Update: Functionality Expansion and ...
results.content: The update process itself has been improved to minimize disruption and ensure reliability. OpenClaw now supports in-place upgrades that preserve user configurations and installed Skills, eliminating the need to manually reconfigure after updates. Rollback capabilities provide a safety net if issues arise, enabling rapid return to previous stable versions. These improvements reduce the operational burden of maintaining current software versions. [...] Looking forward, the roadmap for OpenClaw development emphasizes continued functionality expansion alongside stability and performance improvements. Planned features include enhanced multi-language support, more sophisticated workflow orchestration capabilities, and expanded integration options. The modular Skills architecture ensures that new capabilities can be added without disrupting existing functionality, maintaining stability while growing the platform's utility. [...] The functionality expansion in this update touches multiple aspects of the OpenClaw experience. The core natural language processing engine has been upgraded to support more complex multi-turn conversations with better context retention. Users can now engage in extended dialogues that span multiple topics, with OpenClaw maintaining awareness of previously discussed information and referencing it appropriately in subsequent responses. This enhanced conversational memory transforms the interaction model from a series of isolated queries into coherent, contextual exchanges.

results.title: Updating - OpenClaw
results.content:
For source installs (git checkout), prefer:

Copy

```
openclaw update
```

It runs a safe-ish update flow:
   Requires a clean worktree.
   Switches to the selected channel (tag or branch).
   Fetches + rebases against the configured upstream (dev channel).
   Installs deps, builds, builds the Control UI, and runs `openclaw doctor`.
   Restarts the gateway by default (use `--no-restart` to skip).

If you installed via npm/pnpm (no git metadata), `openclaw update` will try to update via your package manager. If it can’t detect the install, use “Update (global install)” instead.
​

Update (Control UI / RPC) [...] Update (Control UI / RPC)

The Control UI has Update & Restart (RPC: `update.run`). It:
1.   Runs the same source-update flow as `openclaw update` (git checkout only).
2.   Writes a restart sentinel with a structured report (stdout/stderr tail).
3.   Restarts the gateway and pings the last active session with the report.

If the rebase fails, the gateway aborts and restarts without applying the update.
​

Update (from source)

From the repo checkout:Preferred:

Copy

```
openclaw update
```

Manual (equivalent-ish):

Copy

```
git pull
pnpm install
pnpm build
pnpm ui:build # auto-installs UI deps on first run
openclaw doctor
openclaw health
``` [...] ##### Advanced

   Development Channels

On this page

   Updating
   Recommended: re-run the website installer (upgrade in place)
   Before you update
   Update (global install)
   Core auto-updater (optional)
   Update (openclaw update)
   Update (Control UI / RPC)
   Update (from source)
   Always Run: openclaw doctor
   Start / stop / restart the Gateway
   Rollback / pinning (when something breaks)
   Pin (global install)
   Pin (source) by date
   If you’re stuck

Maintenance

Updating

​

Updating

OpenClaw is moving fast (pre “1.0”). Treat updates like shipping infra: update → run checks → restart (or use `openclaw update`, which restarts) → verify.
​

Recommended: re-run the website installer (upgrade in place)

results.title: update - OpenClaw
results.content:
High-level:
1.   Requires a clean worktree (no uncommitted changes).
2.   Switches to the selected channel (tag or branch).
3.   Fetches upstream (dev only).
4.   Dev only: preflight lint + TypeScript build in a temp worktree; if the tip fails, walks back up to 10 commits to find the newest clean build.
5.   Rebases onto the selected commit (dev only).
6.   Installs deps (pnpm preferred; npm fallback).
7.   Builds + builds the Control UI.
8.   Runs `openclaw doctor` as the final “safe update” check.
9.   Syncs plugins to the active channel (dev uses bundled extensions; stable/beta uses npm) and updates npm-installed plugins.

​

`--update` shorthand

`openclaw --update` rewrites to `openclaw update` (useful for shells and launcher scripts).
​

See also [...] Release Checklist
   Tests

##### Experiments

   Onboarding and Config Protocol
   ACP Thread Bound Agents
   Unified Runtime Streaming Refactor Plan
   Browser Evaluate CDP Refactor
   OpenResponses Gateway Plan
   PTY and Process Supervision Plan
   Session Binding Channel Agnostic Plan
   Workspace Memory Research
   Model Config Exploration

On this page

   Git checkout flow
   --update shorthand
   See also

CLI commands

update

​

`openclaw update`

Safely update OpenClaw and switch between stable/beta/dev channels.If you installed via npm/pnpm (global install, no git metadata), updates happen via the package manager flow in Updating.
​

Usage

results.title: OpenClaw Update System And New Features Actually Make Local ...
results.content:
Stable is designed for production environments where reliability matters most.

Beta lets you access newer features earlier while accepting some measured risk.

Dev pulls from the main branch and is meant for users comfortable testing the latest changes.

Switching between these channels is guided through OpenClaw update wizard, which keeps the process simple.

You can also check your current version anytime with OpenClaw update status.

Knowing exactly what you are running reduces upgrade anxiety significantly.

# Security Hardening In OpenClaw New Features

The OpenClaw new features over recent releases show a clear focus on security.

Multiple updates included extensive security related commits instead of just cosmetic changes. [...] OpenClaw new features expand support across multiple model providers so you are not locked in.

Per session routing lets you choose the right model for each task.

Failover options allow workflows to continue even if one provider becomes unavailable.

Per agent parameter overrides improve caching efficiency and reduce redundant API calls.

That directly impacts cost control and stability.

Flexibility is not about having more buttons to click.

It is about reducing risk in real workflows.

# Agent Routing And Threadbound Structure

As automation grows, one assistant responding to everything becomes limiting.

Threadbound agents allow specific agents to attach to defined conversations with lifecycle control.

Startup reconciliation ensures agents reconnect cleanly after restarts. [...] Optional HTTP security headers were added for direct HTTPS deployments.

Browser level SSRF protections were reinforced to reduce exposure.

A plug in and skill safety scanner helps detect unsafe extensions before they become problems.

External secrets management was integrated directly into the CLI workflow.

You can audit and reload credentials without storing API keys in plain text files.

Runtime validation reduces the risk of accidental leaks during configuration changes.

These upgrades show steady maturity rather than short term hype.

# Multi Model Routing Makes OpenClaw Practical

Relying on one provider is fragile over time.

APIs go down, pricing shifts, and performance varies depending on workload.

results.title: Releases · openclaw/openclaw - GitHub
results.content:
CLI/install: include the short git commit hash in `openclaw --version` output when metadata is available, and keep installer version checks compatible with the decorated format. (#39712) thanks @sourman.
   CLI/backup: improve archive naming for date sorting, add config-only backup mode, and harden backup planning, publication, and verification edge cases. (#40163) Thanks @gumadeiras.
   ACP/Provenance: add optional ACP ingress provenance metadata and visible receipt injection (`openclaw acp --provenance off|meta|meta+receipt`) so OpenClaw agents can retain and report ACP-origin context with session trace IDs. (#40473) thanks @mbelinky. [...] CLI/backup: add `openclaw backup create` and `openclaw backup verify` for local state archives, including `--only-config`, `--no-include-workspace`, manifest/payload validation, and backup guidance in destructive flows. (#40163) thanks @shichangs.
   macOS/onboarding: add a remote gateway token field for remote mode, preserve existing non-plaintext `gateway.remote.token` config values until explicitly replaced, and warn when the loaded token shape cannot be used directly from the macOS app. (#40187, supersedes #34614) Thanks @cgdusek. [...] Gateway/Control UI: resolve bundled dashboard assets through symlinked global wrappers and auto-detected package roots, while keeping configured and custom roots on the strict hardlink boundary. (#40385) Thanks @LarytheLord.
   Browser/extension relay: add `browser.relayBindHost` so the Chrome relay can bind to an explicit non-loopback address for WSL2 and other cross-namespace setups, while preserving loopback-only defaults. (#39364) Thanks @mvanhorn.
   Browser/CDP: normalize loopback direct WebSocket CDP URLs back to HTTP(S) for `/json/` tab operations so local `ws://` / `wss://` profiles can still list, focus, open, and close tabs after the new direct-WS support lands. (#31085) Thanks @shrey150.

results.title: OpenClaw Skills
results.content:
### What openclaw skills are

Openclaw skills are the core building blocks of the OpenClaw ecosystem. Each skill package is a folder that contains a SKILL.md file with YAML frontmatter and clear instructions so the agent knows how to use tools, APIs, and scripts. The official OpenClaw skills documentation describes these folders as AgentSkills compatible skill directories that are loaded at startup. Because these skills are stored as plain files, teams can version them, review them, and evolve them over time while keeping behavior predictable. [...] ClawHub is the public registry for openclaw skills. The docs show that you can install openclaw skills with a command like clawhub install , update all openclaw skills with clawhub update --all, and sync or publish with clawhub sync --all. By default, ClawHub installs into ./skills under the current working directory and OpenClaw picks them up on the next session. This directory mirrors those flows so you can search openclaw skills on the web, then run the exact CLI command in your environment.

### Choosing and maintaining openclaw skills [...] Our directory exists to help you find openclaw skills that are popular, useful, and well documented. Instead of hunting through many repositories, you can browse openclaw skills by category, compare what each one does, and decide which openclaw skills fit your workflow. The goal is not to replace the OpenClaw docs, but to make openclaw skills easier to discover and easier to evaluate before you install anything. If you see Clawdbot or Moltbot in older docs or repos, those are the earlier names for the same project now called OpenClaw.

### How openclaw skills are installed and loaded
